highlights.title: Why SpaceX Shares Are Under Pressure
highlights.description: Less than two weeks after the year’s most hotly anticipated IPO, investors are questioning the remaining upside of a company that seeks to raise billions in investor-grade debt as the AI trade stalls and other major offerings from OpenAI and Anthropic loom. Bloomberg's Charlie Wells discusses on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: SpaceX drops more than 3% following $400 billion selloff
highlights.description: Gains have been pared back at the space and AI company following an initial surge after its record-breaking IPO.

highlights.title: India and China are home to 2.9 billion people – and together they bought just 13 million PCs in Q1
highlights.description: PLUS: Indian telco ponders broadband satellites; Samsung goes all-in on OpenAI; Vietnam centrally plans ten tech giants; and more!

highlights.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
highlights.description: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.

highlights.title: OpenAI's new Daybreak⁠ initiative will help open-source projects fend off bugs
highlights.description: OpenAI has launched Patch the Planet, a new initiative part of its Daybreak cybersecurity program.

highlights.title: Masayoshi Son Dismisses Musk’s Idea for Orbital Data Centers
highlights.description: SoftBank Group Corp. founder Masayoshi Son said there’s little merit to building data centers in space, as championed by Elon Musk, predicting that the AI race will be clinched by computing resources on Earth.

highlights.title: SpaceX Fall Continues, Erasing Billions in Market Value
highlights.description: SpaceX shares slipped for a third straight day, shedding hundreds of billions of dollars in market value, after the company said it is selling investment-grade bonds for the first time. Bloomberg's Anthony Stephens and Norah Mulinda break down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: 9to5Mac Daily: June 22, 2026 – iPhone 18 Pro prices, Apple Store closures
highlights.description:
Listen to a recap of the top stories of the day from 9to5Mac. 9to5Mac Daily is available on iTunes and Apple’s Podcasts app, Stitcher, TuneIn, Google Play, or through our dedicated RSS feed for Overcast and other podcast players.
Sponsored by Roborock: Check out Roborock’s Prime Day Sale featuring up to $1,500 off best-selling robot vacuums and wet dry vacuums, including the Saros 10R, Qrevo Edge 2, Qrevo Curv 2 Flow, and more.

highlights.title: Apple's £3 Billion UK iCloud Case Cleared for Trial
highlights.description:
A class action lawsuit accusing Apple of overcharging U.K. iCloud users has been certified to go ahead, putting the £3 billion ($3.9 billion) claim on track for a trial in October 2028.


According to BBC News, the Competition Appeal Tribunal cleared consumer group Which? to bring the case on behalf of an estimated 40 million U.K. iPhone and iPad owners, each of whom could receive up to £77 if the claim succeeds.

limiting how rival cloud services work on its devices, and then charged inflated subscription prices as a result. Apple gives users 5GB of free storage and pushes them toward paid tiers once that fills up, with U.K. pricing running from 99p a month for 50GB to £54.99 a month for 12TB.

November 2024.

Anabel Hoult, Which?'s chief executive, said the group wanted to make clear that no company "no matter how powerful, can get away with abusing its position." She said the green light from the tribunal meant Which? was "one step closer to getting consumers the redress we believe they are owed from Apple."

"This should send a strong message to any other companies using anti-competitive tactics," she added.

Apple has called the claims unfounded, and argues that no customer is required to use iCloud and that alternatives exist. The company said it strongly disagrees with the tribunal's decision and plans to appeal.

Eligibility covers anyone who used iCloud on a U.K. device between November 8, 2018 and June 8, 2026. Those living in the U.K. on June 8 are included automatically unless they opt out by October 8, while non-U.K. residents from that date must opt in by the same deadline. Customers who first used iCloud after June 8, 2026 are excluded.

(Thanks, Alan!)
Tags: iCloud, Apple Lawsuits, United Kingdom
Related Forum: Apple Music, Apple Pay/Card, iCloud, Fitness+
This article, "Apple's £3 Billion UK iCloud Case Cleared for Trial" first appeared on MacRumors.com
Discuss this article in our forums

highlights.title: SpaceX’s $600 billion plunge erased equivalent of nearly half of bitcoin’s market cap in 3 days
highlights.description: The newly public company shed close to half of bitcoin's entire market value in three sessions after announcing its first bond sale. Bitcoin, absorbing the same backdrop, fell less than 1%.

ai.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
ai.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

ai.title: Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 slams into Texas home, killing 76-year-old
ai.description: Harris County authorities said that the driver, Michael Butler, said that he had been using Tesla's partially automated driving systems.

ai.title: US Tech Stocks Set to Slide as AI Rout Hits Global Peers
ai.description: US technology stocks are set to slump on Tuesday after a selloff in Korean chipmakers stoked concerns about the sustainability of the artificial intelligence-related rally.

ai.title: The Oversight Board says Meta needs to do more to protect regular people from sexualized deepfakes
ai.description: Meta's Oversight Board has made several suggestions to make reporting sexualized AI deepfakes easier and more effective for ordinary people.

ai.title: Amazon ordered to bargain with Teamsters in case that could set back union progress
ai.description: Amazon violated federal law when it refused to recognize a union, a judge ruled.

ai.title: India’s Biggest Equity Fund Makes Contrarian IT Stocks Bet
ai.description: India’s largest actively managed equity fund is betting against investor fears over artificial intelligence’s impact on the outsourcing industry as it snaps up the nation’s beaten-down IT stocks.

ai.title: What’s new in Android’s June 2026 Google System Updates [U]
ai.description: The monthly “Google System Release Notes” primarily detail what’s new in Play services, Play Store, and Play system update across Android phones/tablets, Wear OS, Google/Android TV, Auto, and PC. Some features apply to end users, while others are aimed at developers.

ai.title: Trump signs orders to build a quantum computer and protect against the one that could break encryption
ai.description: The White House issued twin executive orders to accelerate U.S. development of large-scale quantum computers while simultaneously hardening defenses against such machines.

business.title: SpaceX drops more than 3% following $400 billion selloff
business.description: Gains have been pared back at the space and AI company following an initial surge after its record-breaking IPO.

business.title: Hut 8 to pay $2.35 million to settle investor suit over U.S. Bitcoin merger
business.description: The former bitcoin miner denied any wrongdoing related to its 2023 merger with U.S. Bitcoin Corp.

chips_cloud.title: The Hidden Costs of Leveraged ETFs
chips_cloud.description: A leveraged ETF linked to SK Hynix has grown larger than Hong Kong's oldest ETF, which tracks the Hang Seng Index. The milestone highlights investors' voracious appetite for risky bets on the booming semiconductor sector. However, Bloomberg Intelligence says products such as the SK Hynix 2x Leveraged ETF carry a hidden structural drag that could erode investor returns over time. (Source: Bloomberg)

space.title: SpaceX’s $600 billion plunge erased equivalent of nearly half of bitcoin’s market cap in 3 days
space.description: The newly public company shed close to half of bitcoin's entire market value in three sessions after announcing its first bond sale. Bitcoin, absorbing the same backdrop, fell less than 1%.

space.title: With Starfall, SpaceX eyes an edge in global cargo delivery from orbit
space.description: The purpose of Starfall is to support the "transport and delivery of goods through space."

space.title: SpaceX Rout Set to Push Market Value Below $2 Trillion
space.description: SpaceX shares extended declines in premarket trading, a day after the company saw the second-largest single-session drop in market value on record.

ev_autonomous.title: EU to Escalate Meta Probe Into Addictive Design That Hooks Kids
ev_autonomous.description: The European Union is set to ramp up an investigation into Meta Platforms Inc. that alleges its products are addictive to children, as the bloc increases regulatory pressure on the US social media company.

ev_autonomous.title: Amazon Ordered to Collectively Bargain at San Francisco Warehouse
ev_autonomous.description: A US labor board judge ruled that Amazon.com Inc. must collectively bargain with California warehouse workers, teeing up a legal battle that could end with President Donald Trump’s appointees reversing a major union-friendly precedent.

ev_autonomous.title: Apple @ Work Podcast: WWDC 26 Recap
ev_autonomous.description:
Apple @ Work is exclusively brought to you by Mosyle, the only Apple Unified Platform. Mosyle is the only solution that integrates in a single professional-grade platform all the solutions necessary to seamlessly and automatically deploy, manage & protect Apple devices at work. Over 45,000 organizations trust Mosyle to make millions of Apple devices work-ready with no effort and at an affordable cost. Request your EXTENDED TRIAL today and understand why Mosyle is everything you need to work with Apple.
In this episode of Apple @ Work, Alcyr Araujo from Mosyle joins the show to talk about all the IT related announcements at WWDC.

ev_autonomous.title: Europe Sees 6 Billion Reasons for New Levy on Online Shoppers
ev_autonomous.description: Bargain-hunting consumers across the European Union will start feeling the pinch of higher online shopping costs next week when a new levy primarily targeting China comes into force, adding a layer of trade friction between the two economies.

regulation.title: Prosus Develops OpenClaw Rival to Address Privacy Concerns
regulation.description: Prosus NV has developed its own version of the artificial intelligence agent OpenClaw to get around European concerns over data privacy, and will start rolling it out this week.
